description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide, slightly rusted metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood is dark brown with visible grain and subtle texturing, giving it a weathered, robust appearance. The metal bands are gray with patches of rust, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top of the barrel is open, revealing the interior, which appears empty and unfinished. The overall shape is slightly bulged at the center, typical of traditional barrels, and the ends of the staves are uneven at the rim. This object would be commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near workshops.
description: A weathered, dark brown wooden barrel with two rusted metal hoops and an open top, showing a classic bulged shape and visible wood grain.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the open top and the uneven ends of the wooden staves.
description_view_1: This view shows the barrel from the side, emphasizing the curvature of the staves and the rusted metal hoops.
description_view_2: The barrel is viewed straight on, displaying the symmetry of the metal bands and the central bulge.
description_view_3: A similar side view to view 1, but with slightly different lighting, showing the texture of the wood and the open top.
